title: The One With The Ballroom Dancing

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, the gang is there eating breakfast. Chandler is cleaning out his wallet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! New wallet, huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it was time. The old condom ring in the leather just doesn't say 'cool' anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You just put an empty carton back in the fridge!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, I know, but the garbage was full.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Have you ever taken out the trash? (Hands her the garbage.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I thought you liked doing it. (Rachel starts out the door and stops.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Third door on the left.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right!
Garbage room: Mr. Treeger is unclogging the trash chute as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Hey, Mr. Treeger.
Mr. Treeger:: Hey.

Rachel opens the trash chute, winces at the smell, and throws the garbage bag in. She then tries to throw the pizza box in, but since it's so big she jams it into the opening and it prevents the door from closing. She then turns around too see Mr. Treeger watching her.
Mr. Treeger:: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ummm. Oh! I'm sorry. (She grabs the box and offers him a piece.) It's a little old but...
Mr. Treeger:: No! You're clogging up the chute that I spent a half-hour unclogging!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry. I didn't-I don't come in here a lot.
Mr. Treeger:: Oh yeah, of course you don't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No.
Mr. Treeger:: 'Cause you're a little princess! "Daddy, buy me a pizza. Daddy, buy me a candy factory. Daddy, make the cast of Cats sing Happy Birthday to me..."

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn't... I never said that.
Mr. Treeger:: You think you could make a mess and the big man in coveralls will come in here and clean it up, huh? Well, why don't think of someone else for a change?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to cry) Okay, I'm sorry. (Runs out still carrying the pizza box.)
[Cut to Monica and Rachel's apartment as Rachel returns in tears.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God! If you're gonna cry about it! (She grabs the box and goes to through it out.]
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Continued from earlier, Rachel is now telling everyone of her experience in the garbage room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, Treeger made you cry?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! And he said really mean things that were only partly true.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm gonna go down there and teach that guy a lesson.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, please don't do that. I think it's best that we just forget about it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's easy for you to say, you weren't almost just killed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right that's it, school is in session! (Exits and slams the door.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Picking up a card from Chandler's wallet.) My God! Is this a gym card?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, gym member. I try to go four times a week, but I've missed the last 1200 times.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So why don't you quit?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You don't think I've tried? You think I like having 50 dollars taken out of my bank account every month? No, they make you go all the way down there! Then they use all of these phrases and peppiness to try and confuse you! Then they bring out Maria.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Who is Maria?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh Maria. You can't say no to her, she's like this lycra spandex covered gym...treat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You need me to go down there with you and hold your hand?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you're strong enough to face her on your own?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no, you'll have to come.
Treeger's apartment, Joey knocks on the door and Treeger opens it.
Mr. Treeger:: Tribbiani! Hold on, I'll get the plunger.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! You hold on pal! Now you made my friend, Rachel, cry. So now, you're gonna go up there and apologize to her, unless you want me to call the landlord.
Mr. Treeger:: And tell him what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Have you heard about a little something called, Not Making Girls Cry.
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah. Well maybe you have heard about the Rent Stabilization Act of 1968!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I have actually not heard of that.

Mr. Treeger:: Yeah, well your friends are in violation of it. I've been a nice guy up until now, but uh, I don't need this grief. I'm gonna call the landlord and tell him that Monica is illegally subletting here grandmother's apartment. Your friends are outta here pal.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why don't you tell me something I don't know! (He storms out, and once Treeger closes the door behind him, Joey makes an 'Oops!' have.)
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier, everyone is still eating breakfast.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh please, somebody tell me I don't have to go to work today!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, my first massage today is this incredibly gorgeous guy, and every time I see him I just want to do things to him that I'm not allowed to charge for.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So do them for free.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, it is forbidden! No-no, Mrs. Potter fires people for fooling around with clients. And it's against my oath as a masseuse.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They make you take an oath?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I made myself take an oath. Yeah, no fooling around with clients and umm, always be prepared. Yeah, that one's actually from the Boy Scouts, but it just makes good sense.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why don't you just give him to somebody else?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I can handle it. No, I'm a professional. (She starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Pheebs, is that a new ankle bracelet?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! And you got a petticure. Your feet are all dressed up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because that's the only part of you he can see when he's on the table!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You're gonna do some feet flirtin'!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't what your talking about. (Laughs nervously and continues to leaqve)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Then how do you explain the toe ring?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Because it's Arabian princess day at work! Okay?! Leave me alone!
[Cut to later, Joey is returning from talking to Mr. Treeger.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! My hero! What happened?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well uh, I went down there and told him that no one treats my friends like that and that he'd better come up here and apologize. I'll see you later. (Starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What a minute, what did he say?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: He said that he wasn't gonna apologize because you guys are living here illegally, so instead what he's gonna do is have you evicted-I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! You got us evicted!!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I told you not to go down there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well he made Rachel cry!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel always cries!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's not true! (Starts to cry.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now Joey, you go down there and you suck up to him. I mean you suck like you've never sucked before!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right! I'll try! But if I can't, you can stay with Chandler and I until you get settled.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Go!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, all right, all right. (Starts to leave, stops, and turns around) I mean I'll have to check with him first, but I'll think he'll be cool with it. (Monica shoos him out.)
Chandler's gym, He and Ross are there to cancel his membership.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, hey! Now remember what we talked about, you gotta be strong.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. (In a stronger voice) Yes!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: One more time, "Hey, don't you want a washboard stomach and rock hard pecs?"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No! I want a flabby gut and saggy man breasts!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Good! That's good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay. (They go inside) (To the guy at the desk) I wanna quit the gym.

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: You wanna quit?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wanna quit the gym.

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: You do realize that you won't have access to our new full service Swedish spa.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (He turns to Ross and Ross makes a 'Be strong' sound.) I wanna quit the gym.

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: Okay, Dave in the membership office, handles quitters. (Both Chandler and Ross start to make their way to the membership office.) Uh, excuse me, (to Ross) are you a member?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Me? No.

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: Sorry, members only.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (horrified at the prospect of trying to quit alone and unsure about himself) I wanna quit the gym.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's okay man, be strong. (Chandler goes into the office.)

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: (to Ross) So, are you a member of any gym.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! And I'm not gonna be, so you can save you little speech.

lines.speaker: Gym Employee
lines.text: Okay, no problem. (To someone out of the picture) Could you come here for a second?
This gorgeous woman in spandex walks up

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, I'm Maria.
Ross is at a loss for words.
Heeling Hands Inc., Phoebe's work, she is giving a massage to the guy, Rick, she likes.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: (looking at her feet) Wow, you have really pretty feet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: These old things.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Would you mind spending some time on my siadic area, it's been killing me today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You mean the-Okay by siadic, you mean the towel covered portion.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure, yeah, no I can do that, yeah, because umm, y'know, the muscles in the siadic area can get y'know, real (lifts up the towel) nice and tight. So umm, tell me Rick, how umm, how did you injure the area.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Oh, a 16-hour sit-in for Greenpeace.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (She goes to work, and her head slowly drops out of view.)

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Ow! Did you just bite me?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No!
Mr. Treeger's apartment, Joey is there to suck up.
Mr. Treeger:: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Please don't kick Monica and Rachel out, this wasn't there fault, it was mine.
Mr. Treeger:: You want me to kick you guys out instead?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No you can't do that, where would the chick and the duck live?
Mr. Treeger:: You have pets!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Noo-no-no, no, those are nicknames. I'm the chick and Chandler is the duck.
Mr. Treeger:: Huh, I would've thought it was the other way around.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man, just-just let the girls stay, I'll do whatever you want.
Mr. Treeger:: Really? You'll do anything?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah-yeah, absolutely.
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah, I've got something you can do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What, what is it?
Mr. Treeger:: Can you be my dancing partner?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's not, prison lingo, is it?
Commercial Break
Central Perk, Joey is telling Monica and Rachel what he has to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: His dancing partner?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, there's this superintendent's dance, the Super Ball. I don't know, and he wants to impress Marge, this lady super that he's a crush on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, why doesn't he practice with a girl?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, he's too shy, he doesn't thing he's good enough to dance with girls yet.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, right, he almost danced me right down that...garbage chute. (Starts to cry)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, would you let it go already?! You're fine!
Chandler and Ross enter

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! So, did you quit?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I almost did, couldn't leave Ross there without a spotter!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, now so you joined the gym?
Rachel starts to laugh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that's funny, why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, umm, I was just y'know working out and umm... Oh, that's it.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We're doomed. Okay, they're gonna take 50 bucks out of our accounts for the rest of our lives. What are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you could actually go to the gym.
Chandler and Ross both laugh

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Or! Or, we could go to the bank, close our accounts and cut them off at the source.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're a genius!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aww, man, now we won't be bank buddies!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, there's two reasons.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh, you guys, remember that cute client I told you about? I bit him.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Where?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: On the touchy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And that's not against your oath?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I know! I-I'm sorry, but the moment I touch him, I just wanna throw out my old oath and take a new, dirty one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, next time your massaging him, you should try and distract yourself.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah! Yeah! Like-like when I'm doing something exciting and I don't wanna get too excited, I just ahh, y'know try to thing of other things like ah sandwiches, and ah baseball, and ah Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, thank you.
Treeger's apartment, Joey knocks and Mr. Treeger opens the door.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, I'm here, let's ahh, get this over with.
Mr. Treeger:: Okay ahh, well, just ahh, follow my lead. (Turns on some music

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, don't we need to do some kinda preparation first? Like ahh, get really drunk?
Mr. Treeger:: Look come on, eh, just ah, just ah, put your arms around me, eh.
Joey does so, and they both start dancing. Treeger tries to spin Joey, but ends up throwing him into the door.
Mr. Treeger:: Ahhhh! I'm sorry!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, it's okay, but if I'm Marge, my breasts are coming out my back.
Mr. Treeger:: Ahh, forget it! I'll never be any good at this, my mom was right, I'm just a big potato with arms, and legs, and a head.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on man, you're not a potato.
Mr. Treeger:: I'm sure as hell a dancer, it's no use Marge will never go for me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on Treeger, don't say that. You just ahh, you just need more practice. Here, come on, let's ahh, let's try it again. Come on. (they start dancing again) Plus, it was, it was probably mostly my fault, anyway. I mean, y'know, I'm not really that comfortable dancing with a-(Treeger throws him) We-he!! Hey!
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah!
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Monica are sitting at the table as Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey-hey, how goes the dancing? Gay yet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah-ha-ha, you guys owe me big time. (He walks into the kitchen and does a little dance step on the way.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughing) What was that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You just did a little dancy thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No I didn't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes you did! You did like a little hop.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You are soo enjoying this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm not! And it wasn't a hop it was a pademarie.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughing harder) You know the words! You are so into this!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, well maybe I'm enjoying it a little bit. I mean I'm getting pretty good at it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, this is soo sweet, Joey our little twinkle-toes.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey, hold on, this isn't some kind of like girly dance. All right, it's like a sport, it's manly!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, then show me some manly moves.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.
They both get up and Monica expects Joey to take the lead, but he doesn't, and they fumble around for a little bit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know how to lead.
Ross and Chandler's bank, they are there to close their accounts.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We'd like to close our accounts.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Close your accounts? Is there some kind of problem?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No-no.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we'd just like to close them.

lines.speaker: Bank Officer
lines.text: Okay, Ms. Lambert handles all our closures. (to a beautiful woman) Would you come over here please?

lines.speaker: Ms. Lambert
lines.text: Hi, I'm Karen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I wanna quit the bank!
Healing Hands, Inc., Phoebe is giving Rick a massage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (thinking to herself) Okay, baseball. Rick, playing baseball. Okay, slides into second, maybe even his pants come down a little... Oh no-wait no, no! No! Okay, all right, sandwiches, sandwiches. Umm, okay, on a plate, maybe Rick's pants come down a little. No! No! Okay, Chandler! Okay Chandler, ooh, that's working.
The camera zooms in on the clock on the wall and it reads a quarter after one. Time lapse. The clock now reads 3:30, and Phoebe is still giving Rick his massage.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (thinking to herself) Chandler's knees. Chandler's... ankles. Chandler's ankle hair. (notices the clock) Oh no. (to Rick) Okay, you're all set.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Oh wow! That was amazing, was that really just an hour?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! In... really long hour world.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh, okay, I have an enormous crush on you. But because you're a client, I can't ask you out, even though you give me y'know, the feeling.

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Wow! I had no idea! But you know, I could always find another masseuse.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Yeah, really.
They start to kiss, then Rick stops suddenly.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rick
lines.text: Suddenly, I very aware that I'm naked.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (laughs) Okay, quit down. (they start to kiss again)
Suddenly, Phoebe's boss, Mrs. Potter, and a client, Mr. Simon, enters.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Potter
lines.text: Mr. Simon's been waiting for-(sees Phoebe and Rick) Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Mr. Simon
lines.text: Why wasn't I offered that? I'd definitely pay more for that.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Potter
lines.text: Phoebe, we have rules here, this isn't that kind of place.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, oh and I know, but this isn't what it looks like, 'cause Rick is my ahh, husband.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Potter
lines.text: Oh really? Well, then you'd better tell his other wife, 'cause she called three times asking where he is.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, I will tell her.
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler and Ross are telling Joey, Rachel, and Monica of their bank woes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So you didn't leave the bank?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No! And somehow, we ended up with a joint checking account.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What are you ever gonna use that for?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To pay for the gym.
Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! So I had a great day, Rick and I really hit it off, and we started making out, and then my boss walked in and fired me for being a whore.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You got fired?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my Gosh!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's so weird, I have never been fired from anything before!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sweety...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just-I just started walking around not knowing what to do next, y'know? I-I started asking people on the street if they wanted massages. Then these policemen, thought I was a whore too. It's been a really bad day, whore wise.
There's a knock on the door and Chandler answers it.
Mr. Treeger:: Hey Duck, is Chick here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah... Bunny-rabbit.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Mr. Treeger) So you ah, ready for our last practice?
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah, but y'know, I think the reason we're not getting that spin right is because my apartment's too small.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, you wanna use our place?
Mr. Treeger:: No, I ahh, had another idea.
[Cut to the roof, where Joey and Mr. Treeger are dancing happily to ^Night and Day^.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: We did it!!
Mr. Treeger:: I know, we did it!! Hey, that was incredible, huh?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, it was amazing! I mean, we totally nailed it, it was beautiful.
Mr. Treeger:: Thank you, listen, thanks a lot Tribbiani, (checks watch). Oh my God, look at the time, I gotta catch the bus to the ball.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh well, okay, good luck.
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Unless you wanna practice the Foxtrot again? Or-or the Tango?
Mr. Treeger:: Ahh, thanks but no. You see I-I think I'm ready to dance with girls.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay.
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Go get 'em Treeger.
Mr. Treeger:: Right. (Starts to leave) Hey, ahh, you wanna come? Marge has a girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (intrigued) Really?
Mr. Treeger:: Yeah, you could dance real good with her, she's the same size as me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, I'm good.
Treeger leaves, and Joey's dances off.
Closing Credits
Lara and Jeni's Massage, Phoebe is interviewing for a job.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: So it looks like you've got some great experience here. Let's see ahh, reason for leaving last job?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, they thought I was a whore.

lines.speaker: Interviewer
lines.text: Okay, we'll give a call if anything comes up.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great! Thank you very much.
The interviewer watches her leave with an 'Oh my goodness' face.
End
